issues.title: Schedule baseline vs. actual comparison still missing
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts and src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx have no baseline_start, baseline_finish, or float fields. The GanttChart renders only actual bars. Superintendent and PM cannot see slippage. This is a P0 from Cycle 1 (core-workflows-C1-006) with zero progress.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts
FILE: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx
FILE: src/types/api.ts

TASK: Add baseline vs. actual comparison to the schedule module.

1. In src/types/api.ts, extend the ScheduleActivity interface to include: baseline_start: string | null, baseline_finish: string | null, float_days: number | null, is_critical: boolean.

2. In src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts, update getScheduleActivities() to SELECT baseline_start, baseline_finish, float_days, is_critical from the schedule_activities table.

3. In src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx:
   - Render a gray semi-transparent bar behind each activity bar showing baseline_start to baseline_finish.
   - Highlight activities where is_critical === true with a red left border (3px solid #E74C3C).
   - Show float_days as a lighter bar extension after the activity bar.
   - Add a legend at the top: gray=Baseline, colored=Actual, red border=Critical Path.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Gantt rows show two bars: baseline (gray, #E5E7EB) and actual (brand color).
- Critical path activities have red left border.
- Float is visually represented.
- TypeScript compiles with no new `any` casts.
- If baseline_start is null, only the actual bar renders (graceful fallback).

issues.title: Lien waiver workflow has no API endpoints or data model
issues.description: src/components/export/LienWaiverPDF.tsx exists and src/stores/lienWaiverStore.ts exists, but src/api/endpoints/lienWaivers.ts is empty or stub-only. No CRUD, no status tracking per sub per payment period, no auto-generation from pay app approvals. Core-workflows-C1-002 remains partial.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/api/endpoints/lienWaivers.ts
FILE: src/stores/lienWaiverStore.ts
FILE: src/types/api.ts

TASK: Implement the lien waiver CRUD API and connect it to pay app approvals.

1. In src/types/api.ts add LienWaiverRow interface:
   { id: string, project_id: string, subcontractor_id: string, payment_period: string, waiver_type: 'conditional_progress' | 'unconditional_progress' | 'conditional_final' | 'unconditional_final', amount: number, status: 'pending' | 'submitted' | 'received', pay_application_id: string | null, submitted_at: string | null, received_at: string | null, created_at: string }

2. In src/api/endpoints/lienWaivers.ts implement:
   - getLienWaivers(projectId): returns LienWaiverRow[] filtered by project
   - createLienWaiver(projectId, payload): inserts a row
   - updateLienWaiverStatus(id, status, timestamp): updates status + timestamp
   - generateWaiversFromPayApp(projectId, payAppId): queries pay_application_line_items joined to subcontractors and bulk-inserts conditional_progress waivers for each sub

3. In src/stores/lienWaiverStore.ts wire the store actions to call these endpoints.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- getLienWaivers returns typed LienWaiverRow[] with no `any`.
- generateWaiversFromPayApp creates one waiver per subcontractor on a pay app.
- All functions call assertProjectAccess(projectId) before any DB operation.
- Errors are thrown as transformSupabaseError(error).

issues.title: Daily log lacks legally defensible weather API integration and incident fields
issues.description: src/components/dailylog/WeatherCard.tsx was added in Cycle 1 but src/components/forms/CreateDailyLogModal.tsx still has no weather auto-population from an external API, and incident entries have no OSHA incident type, root cause, or corrective action fields. Daily logs are legal documents used in claims. Core-workflows-C1-003 remains partial.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/components/forms/CreateDailyLogModal.tsx
FILE: src/lib/weather.ts
FILE: src/api/endpoints/field.ts

TASK: Make the daily log legally defensible with real weather data and complete incident fields.

1. In src/lib/weather.ts, implement fetchWeatherForDate(lat: number, lon: number, date: string): Promise<{ conditions: string, temp_high: number, temp_low: number, wind_speed: number, precipitation_inches: number, source: string }> using the Open-Meteo free API (https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast). Cache results in localStorage keyed by `weather:{date}:{lat}:{lon}`.

2. In src/components/forms/CreateDailyLogModal.tsx:
   - On modal open, call fetchWeatherForDate with the project's lat/lon (from project record) and today's date. Auto-populate weather fields. Show a small 'Weather auto-filled' badge.
   - Add an Incidents section with fields: incident_type (select: Near Miss, First Aid, Recordable, Lost Time, Fatality), description (textarea), injured_party (text), root_cause (text), corrective_action (text), osha_recordable (checkbox).

3. In src/api/endpoints/field.ts, update createDailyLog to persist weather_source string and incident array as JSONB.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Weather fields auto-fill on modal open when project has coordinates.
- Incident form captures all OSHA-required fields.
- weather_source is stored so legal record shows data provenance.
- TypeScript strict, no new `any`.

issues.title: Widespread `any` casts in AI and budget endpoints remain unfixed
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/ai.ts line `(data as any)?.created_at` and src/api/endpoints/budget.ts multiple implicit any casts in mapChangeOrderRow remain from Cycle 1 (core-workflows-C1-004). TypeScript strict mode is not enforced, creating runtime risk in financial calculations.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/api/endpoints/ai.ts
FILE: src/api/endpoints/budget.ts
FILE: src/types/api.ts

TASK: Eliminate all `any` casts in the AI and budget endpoints.

1. In src/api/endpoints/ai.ts:
   - Define interface AiInsightMetaRow { created_at: string } in src/types/api.ts.
   - Replace `(data as any)?.created_at` with a typed cast using the new interface.
   - Ensure all .map() callbacks on Supabase rows use typed row interfaces, not implicit any.

2. In src/api/endpoints/budget.ts:
   - Audit every function for `as any` or implicit any. Replace with typed interfaces already in src/types/api.ts (BudgetItemRow, ChangeOrderRow).
   - mapChangeOrderRow must accept ChangeOrderRow (not any) and return MappedChangeOrder.
   - Where Supabase join results create union types, define a proper intersection interface.

3. Add `// @ts-strict-check` comment at top of both files and resolve all resulting errors.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `grep -n 'as any' src/api/endpoints/ai.ts` returns zero results.
- `grep -n 'as any' src/api/endpoints/budget.ts` returns zero results.
- `tsc --noEmit` passes with zero new errors.
- No runtime behavior changes.

issues.title: RFI and submittal list views have no bulk actions or row-click navigation
issues.description: src/pages/RFIs.tsx and src/pages/Submittals.tsx render tables without checkbox-based bulk selection, no bulk reassign/status-change action bar, and table rows are not clickable to detail views. Core-workflows-C1-009 is not fixed. This breaks the PM's daily workflow completely.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/pages/RFIs.tsx
FILE: src/pages/Submittals.tsx
FILE: src/components/shared/BulkActionBar.tsx

TASK: Add row-click navigation and bulk actions to RFI and Submittal list views.

1. In src/pages/RFIs.tsx:
   - Add a checkbox column as the first column of the table. Use React state `selectedIds: Set<string>`.
   - When selectedIds.size > 0, render BulkActionBar at the bottom of the screen with actions: 'Reassign' (opens a user picker), 'Change Priority' (opens a select), 'Export PDF'.
   - Make every table row clickable: `onClick={() => navigate(`/projects/${projectId}/rfis/${rfi.id}`)}` with cursor-pointer.
   - Add keyboard navigation: useTableKeyboardNavigation hook (already exists at src/hooks/useTableKeyboardNavigation.ts) on the table element.

2. Repeat the same pattern for src/pages/Submittals.tsx with actions: 'Reassign Reviewer', 'Change Status', 'Export'.

3. In src/components/shared/BulkActionBar.tsx, ensure the bar animates up from the bottom (translateY transition 150ms) and shows count of selected items.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Clicking any RFI row navigates to its detail view.
- Selecting 2+ rows shows BulkActionBar.
- Bulk reassign updates all selected records in a single API call.
- Keyboard J/K navigate rows, Space selects, Enter opens detail.
- No TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Portfolio metrics query has no ErrorBoundary, crashes entire dashboard on failure
issues.description: src/pages/Portfolio.tsx fetches metrics for all projects. If any single project metric query throws, the entire portfolio view unmounts with no recovery. Core-workflows-C1-010 shows ErrorBoundary.tsx has minimal changes. A CFO reviewing the board deck sees a blank screen.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/pages/Portfolio.tsx
FILE: src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx

TASK: Wrap portfolio metrics in an ErrorBoundary and add per-card error states.

1. In src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx, ensure the component accepts a `fallback` prop of type ReactNode and an optional `onError` callback. If not present, add them.

2. In src/pages/Portfolio.tsx:
   - Wrap the entire metrics grid in `<ErrorBoundary fallback={<PortfolioErrorFallback />}>`.
   - Create a PortfolioErrorFallback component inline: shows a warning icon, message 'Portfolio data temporarily unavailable. Some projects may have sync issues.', and a 'Retry' button that calls `window.location.reload()`.
   - For each individual project card, wrap in its own `<ErrorBoundary fallback={<ProjectCardError projectId={id} />}>` so one bad project does not hide the rest.
   - Add `onError` to the outer boundary that calls `captureException` from src/lib/errorTracking.ts.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Throwing inside a project card renders that card's error state without affecting other cards.
- The outer ErrorBoundary catches any remaining errors and shows the fallback UI.
- captureException is called with the error and projectId context.
- No TypeScript errors.

issues.title: MappedChangeOrder type is defined but budget financials do not use it correctly
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/budget.ts defines MappedChangeOrder with approved_cost, estimated_cost, submitted_cost fields but the financialEngine in src/lib/financialEngine.ts computes Revised Contract Value without summing approved change orders by status. Projected final cost and variance calculations may be incorrect. Core-workflows-C1-001 not fixed.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/lib/financialEngine.ts
FILE: src/api/endpoints/budget.ts
FILE: src/types/financial.ts

TASK: Fix financial engine to correctly compute key metrics using MappedChangeOrder.

1. In src/lib/financialEngine.ts, update computeProjectFinancials to accept changeOrders: MappedChangeOrder[] as a parameter.

2. Implement the following formulas exactly:
   - approvedCOValue = sum of changeOrders where status === 'approved', using approved_cost field
   - pendingCOValue = sum of changeOrders where status === 'pending_review', using submitted_cost field
   - revisedContractValue = originalContractValue + approvedCOValue
   - projectedFinalCost = invoicedToDate + costToComplete
   - variance = revisedContractValue - projectedFinalCost
   - pendingExposure = pendingCOValue (separate field, not included in variance)

3. In src/types/financial.ts, ensure ProjectFinancials interface has: approvedCOValue, pendingCOValue, revisedContractValue, projectedFinalCost, variance, pendingExposure fields typed as number.

4. Update all callers of computeProjectFinancials in src/api/endpoints/budget.ts to pass the change orders array.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- computeProjectFinancials(financials, changeOrders) returns correct values per the formulas above.
- A project with originalContract=1000000, one approved CO of 50000, invoiced=600000, costToComplete=450000 returns variance=-50000.
- No `any` casts introduced.
- Existing tests in src/test/api/budget.test.ts pass.

issues.title: AI Copilot is a separate page instead of a slide-in panel woven into workflows
issues.description: src/pages/AICopilot.tsx exists as a standalone route. Per the module spec, the AI Copilot must be a slide-in panel from the right, accessible on every page via Cmd+K or a floating button, with context-aware suggested prompts based on the current page. The current implementation breaks the 'AI woven into every action' vision.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/pages/AICopilot.tsx
FILE: src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx
FILE: src/App.tsx
FILE: src/stores/copilotStore.ts

TASK: Convert AI Copilot from a page to a global slide-in panel.

1. In src/stores/copilotStore.ts, add state: isOpen: boolean, currentPageContext: string. Add actions: openCopilot(), closeCopilot(), setPageContext(context: string).

2. Create src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx: a fixed right-side drawer (width 420px, full viewport height, z-index 50, dark overlay behind). It should slide in from the right using a CSS transform transition (150ms ease-out). Render the chat interface from src/pages/AICopilot.tsx inside it. Include a close button (X) in the top right.

3. In src/App.tsx, render `<CopilotPanel />` at the root level outside all routes so it persists across navigation.

4. In src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, ensure clicking the button calls copilotStore.openCopilot().

5. In src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts, bind Cmd+K to openCopilot().

6. In each major page (RFIs, Budget, Schedule, DailyLog), call setPageContext('rfis') etc. on mount so the copilot shows context-aware suggested prompts.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Copilot panel slides in from right on any page without navigation.
- Cmd+K opens the panel from anywhere.
- Panel shows different suggested prompts on RFIs page vs Budget page.
- Closing the panel does not lose conversation history.
- No TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Field capture and daily log forms are not optimized for one-handed iPhone use
issues.description: src/components/forms/CreateDailyLogModal.tsx and src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx use standard modal/form patterns with small tap targets and no swipe-to-dismiss. On a job site with gloves, sub-44px targets and complex form navigation make these unusable. The superintendent's core workflow is broken on mobile.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/components/forms/CreateDailyLogModal.tsx
FILE: src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx
FILE: src/components/layout/MobileLayout.tsx

TASK: Optimize daily log and quick capture for one-handed mobile field use.

1. In src/components/forms/CreateDailyLogModal.tsx:
   - On mobile viewports (< 768px), render as a bottom sheet instead of a centered modal. Use `position: fixed; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; border-radius: 16px 16px 0 0` with a drag handle at the top.
   - Ensure all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, selects) have a minimum height of 48px and padding of at least 12px.
   - Add swipe-down gesture to dismiss: listen for touchstart/touchmove/touchend and dismiss if vertical drag > 80px.
   - Use large section headers to allow thumb navigation between Weather, Crew, Equipment, Narrative sections.

2. In src/components/field/QuickCapture.tsx:
   - The primary actions (Photo, Voice, Daily Log) must be rendered as large cards (full-width, 72px height, 18px text) not small buttons.
   - Add haptic feedback on action tap: call `navigator.vibrate(10)` on touch.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- On a 390px viewport, daily log modal renders as a bottom sheet.
- All tap targets are >= 48px tall.
- Swipe down by 80px dismisses the modal.
- QuickCapture actions are full-width cards.
- No layout regressions on desktop (>= 768px renders as centered modal).

issues.title: Submittal re-submission tracking and multi-party review chain not implemented
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/submittals.ts and src/machines/submittalMachine.ts handle basic status transitions but do not track revision numbers, diff between versions, or support sequential multi-party review (Sub → GC → Architect → Engineer). Ball-in-court is a single string field with no enforcement. P0 from founder priorities.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/api/endpoints/submittals.ts
FILE: src/machines/submittalMachine.ts
FILE: src/types/api.ts

TASK: Add revision tracking and multi-party review chain to submittals.

1. In src/types/api.ts, add SubmittalRevision interface: { id: string, submittal_id: string, revision_number: number, submitted_by: string, submitted_at: string, reviewer_id: string | null, reviewer_role: 'gc' | 'architect' | 'engineer', review_status: 'pending' | 'approved' | 'approved_as_noted' | 'revise_resubmit' | 'rejected', review_comments: string | null, reviewed_at: string | null, file_urls: string[] }.

2. In src/api/endpoints/submittals.ts add:
   - getSubmittalRevisions(submittalId): returns SubmittalRevision[] ordered by revision_number desc
   - createSubmittalRevision(submittalId, payload): increments revision_number, sets reviewer_id from review chain config
   - updateRevisionReview(revisionId, status, comments): updates review fields

3. In src/machines/submittalMachine.ts, add states: in_preparation → submitted_to_gc → gc_review → submitted_to_architect → architect_review → approved | revise_resubmit. On revise_resubmit transition, trigger createSubmittalRevision to start a new revision cycle.

4. Add ball_in_court computed field: returns the reviewer_role of the latest pending revision.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- getSubmittalRevisions returns typed SubmittalRevision[] with no `any`.
- Re-submitting a rejected submittal creates a new revision with incremented number.
- State machine enforces the review chain order.
- ball_in_court is always derived from revision data, not a free-text field.

invented_features.title: Predictive Delay Engine — Monday Morning Risk Brief
invented_features.description: Every Monday at 6 AM, an AI-generated brief is pushed to the superintendent's dashboard. It cross-references the 3-week lookahead with weather forecasts, open RFI count by trade, pending submittals blocking procurement, and historical productivity data by day-of-week. Output: a prioritized list of 'Thursday will be at risk because concrete pour overlaps with 60% rain probability and the forming submittal is still pending GC review.' Each risk item has a one-tap action: reschedule, create RFI, or flag for PM. This is the core differentiator vs. Procore, which has no predictive capability.
invented_features.implementation_sketch: New file: src/lib/predictions.ts already exists. Add a weeklyRiskBrief(projectId) function that: (1) calls getScheduleActivities filtered to next 14 days, (2) calls fetchWeatherForDate for each day, (3) queries open RFIs and pending submittals, (4) passes all context to aiService.generateRiskBrief(context). Store result in ai_insights table with type='weekly_brief'. Render in a new DashboardWidget: src/components/dashboard/widgets/WeeklyRiskBriefWidget.tsx.

invented_features.title: Subcontractor Scorecard — Portfolio-Wide Performance Index
invented_features.description: A real-time scorecard visible on the Crews/Directory page that scores each subcontractor across 5 dimensions: on-time delivery rate (from daily log material entries), RFI response time (from rfi.ball_in_court history), first-pass submittal approval rate (from submittal revision count), safety incidents (from daily log incidents), and schedule adherence (from activity completion vs. baseline). The score (0-100) is shown on each subcontractor card and rolled up across all projects in the portfolio. A GC executive can see that their electrical sub scores 91 on Project A but 54 on Project B and investigate.
invented_features.implementation_sketch: New file: src/lib/subcontractorScoring.ts. computeSubScore(subId, projectId) queries rfis, submittals, daily_logs, and schedule_activities filtered by subcontractor. Weighted average: delivery 25%, RFI response 20%, submittal quality 25%, safety 15%, schedule 15%. Expose via new endpoint src/api/endpoints/people.ts: getSubcontractorScorecard(subId). Render in src/pages/Directory.tsx as a score badge on each company row.

invented_features.title: 13-Week Cash Flow Forecasting Engine
invented_features.description: A CFO-grade cash flow model that projects net cash position 13 weeks out. Inputs: approved pay app schedule (billing dates), historical collection speed per owner, committed subcontractor invoices due, payroll run dates, known upcoming material payments, and retainage release schedule. Output: a week-by-week chart showing inflow, outflow, and net position with a warning band when net position drops below a configurable threshold. The CFO can run what-if scenarios: 'What if the owner pays 15 days late?' and see the downstream impact on sub payments and cash position.
invented_features.implementation_sketch: src/lib/financialEngine.ts: add computeCashFlowForecast(projectId, weeks=13). Pull pay app data, committed costs with due dates, and retainage schedule. For each week, calculate inflow (expected collections) and outflow (payments due). Store in a forecast table or compute on-demand. Render in src/components/dashboard/widgets/CashFlowWidget.tsx (file already exists) with a recharts AreaChart showing inflow (green), outflow (red), net (orange line), and threshold band.

competitive_intel: SiteSyncAI scores ahead of Procore on AI integration architecture (fallback chain, captureException, budget insights generation) and data model depth (MappedChangeOrder, AuditLogEntry, LienWaiverPDF). However, Procore's Quick Capture and Agent Builder ship today while SiteSync's field capture lacks legally defensible weather API integration and the Copilot is still a separate page. Fieldwire beats SiteSync on mobile UX — bottom sheets, 44px targets, swipe gestures — all missing here. Autodesk Construction Cloud has no lien waiver or AIA billing native; SiteSync's LienWaiverPDF and G702/G703 components are a genuine differentiator once the backend is wired. The 3 invented features (risk brief, subcontractor scorecard, 13-week cash flow) would represent genuine category invention that no competitor ships today.
summary: The Core Workflows module has solid architectural bones — the client wrapper, projectScopedQuery isolation, typed error handling, and state machines are well-structured — but critical construction domain features remain at stub or partial status after two cycles. The schedule still has no baseline vs. actual comparison (P0 since Cycle 1), lien waivers have a PDF component but zero API backing, and the daily log is not legally defensible without a real weather data source and complete OSHA incident fields. TypeScript quality is undermined by persistent `any` casts in the financial and AI endpoints, which creates risk in the core calculations a CFO would trust. The AI Copilot is architecturally inverted — it must be a global slide-in panel, not a page — and the RFI/submittal workflows lack the bulk actions and row-click navigation that a PM handles 50 times a day. Ten issues are ranked by severity with self-contained fix prompts. Three invented features — the Monday Morning Risk Brief, Portfolio Subcontractor Scorecard, and 13-Week Cash Flow Engine — represent the category-defining capabilities that would make a $500M GC switch overnight.
